title: BlackBerry's Transformation and Growth in Secure Communications
text: BlackBerry Ltd. has successfully completed its turnaround and shifted into growth mode, capitalizing on rising demand for secure defense communications solutions. The Waterloo-based company reported strong fourth-quarter results with revenue increasing 10% to $156 million and adjusted EBITDA surging 71% to $36.1 million. CEO John Giamatteo stated the turnaround is complete, and the BlackBerry story is now a growth story. The QNX embedded systems unit achieved record quarterly revenue of $78.7 million, up 20% year-over-year, maintaining leadership in automotive applications, serving major manufacturers like BMW, Honda, Toyota, and Volkswagen. A significant win involved securing a deal with a tier-one supplier for the Chinese luxury electric vehicle market. The Secure Communications division posted 8% revenue growth to $72.5 million with 55% EBITDA growth. Major contracts include expanded partnerships with Canadian federal agencies through 2033 and sales to the German government. Governments worldwide increasingly prioritize digital sovereignty—maintaining critical communications on domestically-controlled infrastructure. BlackBerry is positioned to capitalize on this geopolitical demand for secure, independent communication networks.
